text: Fill in the blanks on carbohydrate chemistry, metabolism, and diabetes.
questions: Glucose is an *aldohexose/Aldohexose* while fructose is a *ketohexose/Ketohexose*; both have the molecular formula *C6H12O6/C₆H₁₂O₆*.
Glycolysis occurs in the *cytoplasm/cytosol* and converts one molecule of glucose into two molecules of *pyruvate/pyruvic acid*, yielding a net gain of *2/two* ATP molecules.
The rate-limiting enzyme of glycolysis is *phosphofructokinase-1/PFK-1/PFK1*, while the rate-limiting enzyme of gluconeogenesis is *fructose-1,6-bisphosphatase/fructose bisphosphatase*.
*Lactose/Milk sugar* intolerance occurs due to deficiency of the enzyme *lactase/Lactase*, leading to undigested sugar being fermented by colonic bacteria causing bloating and diarrhoea.
*HbA1c/Glycated haemoglobin/glycosylated haemoglobin* reflects average blood glucose over the preceding *2-3 months/3 months/2 to 3 months* and is used to monitor diabetic control.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the material and try again.
Good effort! Review the areas you missed.
Excellent! You have a strong grasp of this topic.
